name: Azure WAF Admin Path Restriction Baseline
description: Deny-by-default baseline policy for sensitive administrative endpoints on Azure Front Door WAF and Application Gateway WAF with explicit trusted source CIDR allowlisting.
objective: Restrict access to sensitive administrative paths so only explicitly trusted source CIDRs can reach them, while blocking all other sources by default.
risk_mitigated: Administrative interface brute-force and credential stuffing exposure
Reconnaissance and attack surface enumeration of privileged endpoints
Unauthorized access attempts to backend management surfaces
Opportunistic exploitation of unpatched admin tooling
assumptions: Administrative endpoints are not intended for broad internet access
Trusted operator IP space can be represented as CIDR ranges
Application path routing preserves request URI path for WAF evaluation
tuning_notes: Replace example CIDRs with enterprise NAT egress ranges, VPN gateways, and break-glass bastion IPs before production rollout.
If admin routes exist under additional prefixes (for example /console, /cms, /staff), add them to admin_path_prefixes.
Keep URI transforms (Lowercase, UrlDecode) enabled to reduce bypass attempts via case or encoding tricks.
For proxied architectures, confirm WAF evaluates the true client IP. If not, align with trusted proxy configuration before enforcement.
If operationally required, create a temporary emergency allowlist entry with expiry process and change control.
deployment_notes: Deploy initially in Detection/Log mode (where supported) to observe legitimate admin traffic patterns.
Validate no business-critical automation accesses blocked admin paths from non-allowlisted ranges.
Promote to Block mode after at least one full business cycle of clean telemetry.
Apply consistently across both Azure Front Door and Application Gateway policies to avoid control gaps.
Document ownership of trusted_source_cidrs and establish an update runbook tied to network changes.
monitoring_guidance: Track blocked request count grouped by RequestUri and client IP to detect reconnaissance spikes.
Alert on sudden increases in blocked attempts to /admin, /wp-admin, /manage, and /internal.
Review top blocked source IPs for threat intel enrichment and potential upstream blocking.
Periodically audit allowlisted CIDRs for stale or overly broad entries.
Correlate WAF blocks with identity provider failed-login telemetry for coordinated attack detection.
false_positive_considerations: Internal monitoring systems or synthetic probes may require explicit allowlisting if they validate admin endpoints.
Shared corporate egress changes can temporarily block administrators until CIDR inventory is updated.
